title: misconception_map
description: Pipeline artifact produced by the Misconception Architect for a game in active design. Captures predicted learner error patterns before the prototype is built. Gate threshold: >= 3 entries with all required fields populated. See docs/os_spec_2026.md Section 4.1.

game_family.description: Interaction family name, e.g. 'Bakery Rush'. From family_architecture_brief.family_name.

interaction_type.description: Primary interaction type driving the misconception predictions.

source_confusion_risks.description: The expected_confusion_risks array from lowest_viable_loop_brief, used as primary seed.

library_reference_used.description: True if an existing artifacts/misconception_library/ entry was cross-referenced.

gate_threshold_met.description: True if >= 3 misconceptions have all required fields. Pipeline gate requires true to advance.

valid_misconception_count.description: Count of misconceptions with all 9 required fields populated.

misconceptions.description: Predicted misconceptions covering all six error categories. Should cover procedure_slip, concept_confusion, representation_mismatch, impulsive_guess, rule_misunderstanding, strategic_overload.

change_rationale.description: Why this entry was kept, revised, or added relative to the seeded library. Present only when a library was cross-referenced.

priority.description: When multiple entries share a category, 'primary' is the original or most authoritative; 'secondary' covers a distinct sub-pattern.

primary_entry_id.description: For secondary entries, the id of the primary entry in the same category.

quality_notes.description: Quality gate findings for model-produced entries. Absent for deterministic entries.

notes.description: Agent reasoning, library cross-reference results, or warnings about coverage gaps.
